response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with an impressive full-stack and systems background, but he does not meet the role's mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. He lists SQL and MySQL but shows no SQL Server or Oracle experience, and his financial exposure (payments at Stripe, investment tooling at RIDE Capital) does not constitute the required fixed-income or capital-markets domain knowledge. His career is centered on TypeScript/Node.js web development and DLang systems work, making him a poor fit for this specialized C++ capital-markets consulting engagement.
strengths: Extensive senior engineering experience at reputable companies (Stripe, Shopify) with strong communication skills demonstrated through talks and workshops
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, caching, services handling millions of requests per second)
Solid engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and DevOps tooling experience
concerns: Only about seven months of professional C++ experience (2014-2015), versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience and no fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain background
Career trajectory is firmly web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), suggesting weak alignment and retention risk for a C++ finance role

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and systems fundamentals
